content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Greg Rolen, Esq. 
General Counsel 
Mt. Diablo Unified School District 
1936 Carlotta Drive 
Concord, California 94519 
December 10, 2012 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. I- 12-164 
Dear Mr. Rolen: 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the Mount Diablo Unified 
School District (the "District") regarding the District's duties under the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 The Commission does not provide formal 
advice to third parties concerning another person's duties under the Act. (Regulation 
18329(b)(8).) Because you have not named the official about whom you request advice, we 
cannot provide formal advice. However, Regulation 18329(c)(l) does provide that informal 
assistance may be requested by any person with a duty to advise other persons relating to their 
duties or actions under the Act. As General Counsel of the District, you have such a duty 
therefore we are providing informal assistance. Informal assistance does not provide the 
requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; 
Regulation 18329( c)(3 ).) 
Please also note that there are other bodies of law, separate and apart from the Act's 
conflict-of-interest provisions that may apply to your situation. Our advice is based solely on the 
Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application of other incompatible activities and 
conflict-of-interest laws that may apply including, but not limited to, common law conflict of 
interest and Government Code Section 1090. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless olherwise indicated. The regulalions of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulalions. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indicated. 


QUESTION2 
File No. l-12-164 
Page No. 2 
May a Board member submit an application for employment with the District while still 
sitting as a Board member? 
CONCLUSION 
The Act does not prohibit a Board member submitting an application for employment 
with the District while still sitting as a Board member. However, the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Act limit the officials conduct in connection with the application as set forth 
below. 
FACTS 
You are the General Counsel at the Mt. Diablo Unified School District. 
One of your District Board members announced that he would not be seeking reelection 
to the Board. The Board member then submitted an application for employment with the 
District. The Board member has no influence over the department for which he applied, except 
for normal Board oversight. The position has not yet been filled, and applications are still being 
accepted. The Board member will be off the Board before the position is offered. 
ANALYSIS 
Conflicts of Interest 
The Act does not contain an outright prohibit on a Board member from applying for 
employment with his or her own agency while serving as a member of the Board. Government 
Code Section I 090 has such a prohibition under certain circumstances, but is not a section of the 
Act. 
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply to "public officials"3 (Sections 87100, 
87103; Regulation 18700(b)(l)) making, participating in, or influencing governmental decisions. 
Your Board member will not be making or participating in the future hiring decision you 
describe, since he will have left office by that time. 
2 You also asked whether the district was prohibited from hiring the Board member as a district employee. 
A conflict of interest under Section 87100, if one were 10 exist, is personal to the public official that has an 
economic interest in a decision and would not be imputed to other Board members or district staff deciding to hire or 
not hire a former Board member as a district employee. 
' A "public official" is "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government 
agency ... " (Section 82048.) 


File No. 1-12-164 
Page No. 3 
"Influencing" is broadly defined and also prohibits certain conduct when a conflict of 
interest exists. "Influencing" is defined in Regulation 18702.J(a) to include any contacts, 
appearances before, or other attempts to influence, any member, officer, employee or consultant 
of the official's agency. However, Regulation 18702.4(b)(l) and (3) provide that an official is 
not attempting to use his or her official position to influence a governmental decision before the 
official's agency if the official: 
• 
Appears in the same manner as any other member of the general public before an agency 
in the course of its prescribed governmental function solely to represent himself or 
herself on a matter which is related to his or her personal interests. 
• 
Negotiates his or her compensation or the terms and conditions of his or her employment 
or contract. 
Revolving Door Prohibition 
Finally, please be aware that some former local officials who leave governmental service 
are subject to the Act's one-year ban for local officials in Section 87406.3. "Generally, this 
restriction prohibits certain former local officials from communicating with their former 
agencies.for compensation and in represelllation of another person, for the purpose of 
influencing any legislative or administrative actions, including quasi-legislative and quasi­
judicial actions, or any discretionary actions involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or 
revocation of a permit, license, grant or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property. 
This section, however, would not bar future employment with the official's former agency. Since 
your question does not implicate this provision we do not analyze it further. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
,,. 
JWW:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
. Wallace 
Assi ant General Counsel 
gal Division
